title: Introduction to mlstm
text: mlstm provides tools fitting: Latent Dirichlet Allocation (LDA) Supervised Topic Models (STM) Multi-output supervised topic models (MLSTM) vignette shows minimal end--end workflow using simulated data.

title: Introduction to mlstm
text: generate small document-term representation triplet form. row count (d, v, c) : d: document index (0-based) v: vocabulary index (0-based) c: token count
code: library(mlstm)  D <- 50 V <- 200 K <- 5  NZ_per_doc <- 20 NZ <- D * NZ_per_doc  count <- cbind(   d = as.integer(rep(0:(D - 1), each = NZ_per_doc)),   v = as.integer(sample.int(V, NZ, replace = TRUE) - 1L),   c = as.integer(rpois(NZ, 3) + 1L) )  Y <- cbind(   y1 = rnorm(D),   y2 = rnorm(D) )  dim(count) #> [1] 1000    3 head(count) #>      d   v c #> [1,] 0 158 3 #> [2,] 0 178 4 #> [3,] 0  13 6 #> [4,] 0 194 4 #> [5,] 0 169 3 #> [6,] 0  49 6 dim(Y) #> [1] 50  2

title: Introduction to mlstm
text: first fit unsupervised LDA model. output typically includes: theta: document-topic proportions phi: topic-word distributions additional trace information depending implementation
code: mod_lda <- run_lda_gibbs(   count = count,   K = K,   alpha = 0.1,   beta = 0.01,   n_iter = 20,   verbose = FALSE )  str(mod_lda$theta) #>  num [1:50, 1:5] 0.00124 0.3283 0.17736 0.0816 0.02819 ... str(mod_lda$phi) #>  num [1:200, 1:5] 1.22e-05 1.22e-05 1.22e-05 2.69e-02 1.22e-05 ...

title: Introduction to mlstm
text: Next, fit supervised topic model using single response variable. available returned object, can also inspect optimization traces ELBO:

title: Introduction to mlstm
text: Finally, fit multi-output supervised topic model. STM, can inspect fitting diagnostics stored returned object.

title: Introduction to mlstm
text: package checks documentation builds, better keep examples vignettes light: use small synthetic datasets keep number iterations modest avoid verbose console output makes vignette suitable local builds, GitHub, CRAN workflows.

what: mlstm: Multilevel Supervised Topic Models with Multiple Outcomes in R
title: mlstm: Multilevel Supervised Topic Models with Multiple Outcomes in R
text: mlstm: Multilevel Supervised Topic Models Multiple Outcomes R

title: mlstm: Multilevel Supervised Topic Models with Multiple Outcomes in R
text: mlstm implements Multilevel Supervised Topic Models (MLSTM), probabilistic framework analyzing text data multiple associated outcome variables. Unlike standard supervised topic models assume single response per document, MLSTM allows multiple outcomes introduces hierarchical regression structure share information across . package provides efficient variational inference algorithms implemented C++ via Rcpp, enabling scalable estimation large text corpora.

title: mlstm: Multilevel Supervised Topic Models with Multiple Outcomes in R
text: Multi-output supervised topic modeling Hierarchical regression structure across outcomes Variational Bayesian inference (fast scalable) Supports missing outcome values C++ backend via RcppParallel performance

title: mlstm: Multilevel Supervised Topic Models with Multiple Outcomes in R
code: # install.packages("remotes") remotes::install_github("thimeno1993/mlstm")

title: mlstm: Multilevel Supervised Topic Models with Multiple Outcomes in R
code: library(mlstm) set.seed(123)  D <- 50 V <- 200 K <- 5  NZ_per_doc <- 20 NZ <- D * NZ_per_doc  count <- cbind(   d = rep(0:(D - 1), each = NZ_per_doc),   v = sample.int(V, NZ, replace = TRUE) - 1L,   c = rpois(NZ, 3) + 1 )  Y <- cbind(   y1 = rnorm(D),   y2 = rnorm(D) )

title: mlstm: Multilevel Supervised Topic Models with Multiple Outcomes in R
code: mod_lda <- run_lda_gibbs(   count = count,   K     = K,   alpha = 0.1,   beta  = 0.01,   n_iter = 20,   verbose = FALSE )  str(mod_lda$theta) str(mod_lda$phi)

title: mlstm: Multilevel Supervised Topic Models with Multiple Outcomes in R
code: y <- Y[, 1]  set_threads(2)  mod_stm <- run_stm_vi(   count = count,   y     = y,   K     = K,   alpha = 0.1,   beta  = 0.01,   max_iter = 50,   min_iter = 10,   verbose  = FALSE )  y_hat <- ((mod_stm$nd / mod_stm$ndsum) %*% mod_stm$eta)[, 1] cor(y, y_hat)

title: mlstm: Multilevel Supervised Topic Models with Multiple Outcomes in R
code: J <- ncol(Y)  mu      <- rep(0, K) upsilon <- K + 2 Omega   <- diag(K)  mod_mlstm <- run_mlstm_vi(   count  = count,   Y      = Y,   K      = K,   alpha  = 0.1,   beta   = 0.01,   mu     = mu,   upsilon = upsilon,   Omega   = Omega,   max_iter = 50,   min_iter = 10,   verbose  = FALSE )  Y_hat <- ((mod_mlstm$nd / mod_mlstm$ndsum) %*% mod_mlstm$eta) cor(Y, Y_hat)

what: One Gibbs sampling sweep for LDA (collapsed) using document–term list. — eLDA_pass_b_fast
title: One Gibbs sampling sweep for LDA (collapsed) using document–term list. — eLDA_pass_b_fast
text: function performs single collapsed Gibbs sampling pass non-zero document–term entries. (d, v, count) triple treated `count` replicated word tokens sharing topic assignment.

title: One Gibbs sampling sweep for LDA (collapsed) using document–term list. — eLDA_pass_b_fast
code: eLDA_pass_b_fast(mod, count, ndsum, NZ, V, K, alpha, beta)

title: One Gibbs sampling sweep for LDA (collapsed) using document–term list. — eLDA_pass_b_fast
text: mod List current sampler state: z, nd, nw, nwsum described . count IntegerMatrix size NZ×3, row triple (d, v, c) 0-based indices: document index d, word index v, count c (doc, word) pair. ndsum IntegerVector length D; total token count per document (.e., ndsum[d] = sum_k nd(d,k)). Updated place. NZ Integer, number non-zero entries (rows count length z). V Integer, vocabulary size. K Integer, number topics. alpha Scalar Dirichlet prior parameter document–topic distributions \(\theta_d\) (symmetric). beta Scalar Dirichlet prior parameter topic–word distributions \(\phi_k\) (symmetric).

title: One Gibbs sampling sweep for LDA (collapsed) using document–term list. — eLDA_pass_b_fast
text: list updated state: z Updated topic assignment vector (length NZ). nd Updated D×K document–topic counts. nw Updated K×V topic–word counts. nwsum Updated total word counts per topic.

title: One Gibbs sampling sweep for LDA (collapsed) using document–term list. — eLDA_pass_b_fast
text: state stored list `mod` containing: z Integer vector length NZ; topic assignment       (d, v, count) triple. nd D×K integer matrix; document–topic counts. nw K×V integer matrix; topic–word counts. nwsum Integer vector length K; total word count per topic.

what: Initialize LDA/STM state from a (d, v, c) sparse count matrix. — init_mod_from_count
title: Initialize LDA/STM state from a (d, v, c) sparse count matrix. — init_mod_from_count
text: Given document-term matrix triplet form (d, v, c) using 0-based indices, function initializes LDA state: - samples initial topic assignments z, - constructs document-topic counts nd, - constructs topic-word counts nw, - computes ndsum, nwsum, normalized topic proportions X.

title: Initialize LDA/STM state from a (d, v, c) sparse count matrix. — init_mod_from_count
code: init_mod_from_count(count, K = NULL, phi = NULL, seed = NULL)

title: Initialize LDA/STM state from a (d, v, c) sparse count matrix. — init_mod_from_count
text: count Integer matrix 3 columns representing triples (d, v, c), d v 0-based indices. K Integer, number topics. Required `phi` NULL. `phi` provided, K inferred ncol(phi). phi Optional numeric matrix size V x K specifying per-word topic probabilities used initialization. seed Optional integer random seed.

title: Initialize LDA/STM state from a (d, v, c) sparse count matrix. — init_mod_from_count
text: list components: z Integer vector (length NZ) sampled topics, 0-based. nd DxK document-topic count matrix. nw KxV topic-word count matrix. ndsum Integer vector (length D) row sums nd. nwsum Integer vector (length K) row sums nw. X DxK matrix normalized topic proportions nd / ndsum. D Number documents. V Vocabulary size. K Number topics. NZ Number non-zero entries (rows count).

title: Initialize LDA/STM state from a (d, v, c) sparse count matrix. — init_mod_from_count
text: topic-word probability matrix `phi` provided (V x K), initial topics sampled according phi[v+1, ]. Otherwise, topics sampled uniformly K topics.

what: Collapsed LDA Gibbs sampling for sparse (d, v, c) triplet data. — run_lda_gibbs
title: Collapsed LDA Gibbs sampling for sparse (d, v, c) triplet data. — run_lda_gibbs
text: function performs collapsed Gibbs sampling standard LDA model using sparse document-term representation: initializes LDA state via init_mod_from_count(), runs n_iter iterations C++ Gibbs kernel         eLDA_pass_b_fast(), returns final model state, including posterior topic-word         document-topic distributions.

title: Collapsed LDA Gibbs sampling for sparse (d, v, c) triplet data. — run_lda_gibbs
code: run_lda_gibbs(   count,   K,   alpha,   beta,   n_iter = 100L,   phi = NULL,   seed = NULL,   verbose = TRUE,   progress_every = 10L )

title: Collapsed LDA Gibbs sampling for sparse (d, v, c) triplet data. — run_lda_gibbs
text: count Integer matrix size NZ x 3 rows (d, v, c) 0-based indexing: document index d, word index v, count c pair. K Integer, number topics. Required unless phi supplied. phi provided, K inferred ncol(phi). alpha Scalar Dirichlet prior parameter document-topic distributions. beta Scalar Dirichlet prior parameter topic-word distributions. n_iter Integer, number Gibbs sweeps run. phi Optional V x K topic-word probability matrix used initializing topic assignments init_mod_from_count(). seed Optional integer random seed passed initializer. verbose Logical; TRUE, print progress messages. progress_every Integer; print progress every many iterations.

title: Collapsed LDA Gibbs sampling for sparse (d, v, c) triplet data. — run_lda_gibbs
text: list mod containing: z Integer vector length NZ; final topic assignments (0-based). nd D x K document-topic count matrix. nw K x V topic-word count matrix. ndsum Integer vector length D; document token counts. nwsum Integer vector length K; topic token counts. phi V x K topic-word posterior mean       \(p(w \mid z=k)\) computed nw. theta D x K document-topic posterior mean       \(p(z=k \mid d)\) computed nd. loglik_trace Vector log-likelihoods. D Number documents. V Vocabulary size. K Number topics. NZ Number non-zero (d, v, c) entries.

what: Multi-level supervised topic model (MLSTM) via variational inference. — run_mlstm_vi
title: Multi-level supervised topic model (MLSTM) via variational inference. — run_mlstm_vi
text: function fits multi-output supervised LDA model hierarchical prior regression coefficients:   $$\eta_j \sim N(\mu, \Lambda^{-1}), \quad \Lambda \sim \text{IW}(\upsilon, \Omega).$$

title: Multi-level supervised topic model (MLSTM) via variational inference. — run_mlstm_vi
code: run_mlstm_vi(   count,   Y,   K,   alpha,   beta,   mu,   upsilon,   Omega,   phi = NULL,   seed = NULL,   max_iter = 200L,   min_iter = 50L,   tol_elbo = 1e-04,   update_sigma = TRUE,   tau = 20L,   exact_second_moment = FALSE,   show_progress = TRUE,   chunk = 5000L,   verbose = TRUE,   sigma2_init = NULL )

title: Multi-level supervised topic model (MLSTM) via variational inference. — run_mlstm_vi
text: count Integer matrix 3 columns (d, v, c), using 0-based indices. row represents document index d, word index v, token count c. Y Numeric matrix size D x J containing J response variables D documents. NA values allowed ignored initial regression used seed eta sigma2. K Integer, number topics. Required phi NULL; ignored phi supplied, case K = ncol(phi). alpha Dirichlet prior parameter document-topic distributions. beta Dirichlet prior parameter topic-word distributions. mu Numeric vector length K; prior mean \(\eta_j\). upsilon Scalar degrees freedom inverse-Wishart prior precision matrix \(\Lambda\). Omega Numeric K x K positive-definite scale matrix inverse-Wishart prior. phi Optional numeric matrix size V x K used initialize topic assignments via init_mod_from_count(). seed Optional integer random seed used initialization. max_iter Maximum number variational sweeps. min_iter Minimum number sweeps checking convergence. tol_elbo Numeric tolerance relative ELBO change used convergence criterion. update_sigma Logical; TRUE, update sigma2 inside stm_multi_hier_vi_parallel(). FALSE, keep sigma2 fixed initialized value. tau Log-space cutoff local topic responsibilities C++ routine (controls pruning stability speed). exact_second_moment Logical; reserved flag intended control whether exact second moment \(E[\bar{z}\bar{z}^\top]\) accumulated E-step. **Currently option effect**: underlying C++ implementation ignores accumulated second-moment matrix updating variational parameters, approximate moment based \(\bar{z}\bar{z}^\top\) effectively used. show_progress Logical; forwarded stm_multi_hier_vi_parallel(). chunk Integer; number documents per parallel block C++ E-step. verbose Logical; TRUE, print ELBO relative change sweep. sigma2_init Optional numeric scalar length-J vector specifying initial noise variances. NULL, sigma2 estimated response dimension least squares regression Y[, j] initial topic proportions.

title: Multi-level supervised topic model (MLSTM) via variational inference. — run_mlstm_vi
text: list mod containing (least): nd D x K document-topic counts. nw K x V topic-word counts. ndsum Integer vector length D; document token counts. nwsum Integer vector length K; topic token counts. eta K x J matrix regression coefficients. sigma2 Length-J vector noise variances. Lambda_E K x K posterior mean \(\Lambda\) (returned C++). IW_upsilon_hat Posterior degrees freedom (returned C++). IW_Omega_hat Posterior scale matrix (returned C++). phi V x K topic-word posterior mean       \(p(w \mid z=k)\) computed nw. theta D x K document-topic posterior mean       \(p(z=k \mid d)\) computed nd. elbo Final ELBO value. label_loglik Final label log-likelihood term. elbo_trace Numeric vector ELBO values iterations. label_loglik_trace Numeric vector label log-likelihoods. n_iter Number sweeps actually performed. D Number documents. V Vocabulary size. K Number topics. J Number response dimensions. NZ Number non-zero (d, v, c) entries.

title: Multi-level supervised topic model (MLSTM) via variational inference. — run_mlstm_vi
text: latent topic layer standard LDA, response dimension j follows Gaussian regression document-level topic proportions. Variational inference performed repeated calls C++ routine stm_multi_hier_vi_parallel() convergence maximum number sweeps reached. Convergence assessed based relative changes evidence lower bound (ELBO) supervised label log-likelihood:   $$     \frac{\mathrm{ELBO}_t - \mathrm{ELBO}_{t-1}}{|\mathrm{ELBO}_{t-1}|},     \qquad     \frac{\ell_t - \ell_{t-1}}{|\ell_{t-1}|}.   $$ minimum number iterations, algorithm declared converged quantities non-negative smaller prescribed tolerance.

what: Supervised topic model (STM) variational inference with ELBO-based convergence. — run_stm_vi
title: Supervised topic model (STM) variational inference with ELBO-based convergence. — run_stm_vi
text: function performs supervised topic model (STM) using variational inference. initializes topic assignments count (optionally using topic-word prior phi), estimates regression parameters, repeatedly calls C++ routine stm_vi_parallel() convergence.

title: Supervised topic model (STM) variational inference with ELBO-based convergence. — run_stm_vi
code: run_stm_vi(   count,   y,   K,   alpha,   beta,   phi = NULL,   seed = NULL,   max_iter = 200L,   min_iter = 50L,   tol_elbo = 1e-04,   update_sigma = TRUE,   tau = 20L,   show_progress = TRUE,   chunk = 5000L,   verbose = TRUE,   sigma2_init = NULL )

title: Supervised topic model (STM) variational inference with ELBO-based convergence. — run_stm_vi
text: count Integer matrix 3 columns (d, v, c) 0-based indexing. row represents document index d, word index v, token count c. y Numeric vector length D. Must contain NA values. K Integer, number topics. Required phi NULL; ignored phi provided (K = ncol(phi)). alpha Dirichlet prior parameter document-topic distributions. beta Dirichlet prior parameter topic-word distributions. phi Optional V x K topic-word probability matrix used initializing topic assignments. seed Optional integer random seed used initialization step. max_iter Maximum number variational sweeps. min_iter Minimum number sweeps checking ELBO convergence. tol_elbo Numeric tolerance relative ELBO change. update_sigma Logical; TRUE, update sigma2 sweep. tau Numeric log-space cutoff used stm_vi_parallel(). show_progress Logical; print low-level progress inside C++. chunk Integer; number documents per parallel block. verbose Logical; print ELBO relative change per sweep. sigma2_init Optional numeric scalar specifying initial noise variance. NULL, sigma2 estimated least squares.

title: Supervised topic model (STM) variational inference with ELBO-based convergence. — run_stm_vi
text: list containing: nd D x K document-topic count matrix. nw K x V topic-word count matrix. ndsum Length-D vector document token counts. nwsum Length-K vector topic token counts. eta K-dimensional regression coefficient vector. sigma2 Final noise variance. phi V x K topic-word posterior mean. theta D x K document-topic posterior mean. elbo Final ELBO. label_loglik Final supervised term. elbo_trace ELBO values per sweep. label_loglik_trace Label log-likelihood per sweep. n_iter Number iterations actually performed. D, V, K, NZ Model dimensions.

title: Supervised topic model (STM) variational inference with ELBO-based convergence. — run_stm_vi
text: Convergence assessed based relative changes evidence lower bound (ELBO) supervised label log-likelihood:   $$     \frac{\mathrm{ELBO}_t - \mathrm{ELBO}_{t-1}}{|\mathrm{ELBO}_{t-1}|},     \qquad     \frac{\ell_t - \ell_{t-1}}{|\ell_{t-1}|}.   $$ minimum number iterations, algorithm declared converged quantities non-negative smaller prescribed tolerance. **Important:** function assumes response vector y contains **NA** values. underlying C++ implementation skip missing responses requires y[d] finite documents.

what: Set threading options for STM/MLSTM computations — set_threads
title: Set threading options for STM/MLSTM computations — set_threads
text: helper configures OpenMP/BLAS threads ensure reproducible stable performance across low-level C++ routines used package.

title: Set threading options for STM/MLSTM computations — set_threads
code: set_threads(num_threads = NULL)

title: Set threading options for STM/MLSTM computations — set_threads
text: num_threads Integer number threads. NULL, use (cores - 1).

what: Variational inference for multi-output supervised topic models with hierarchical prior. — stm_multi_hier_vi_parallel
title: Variational inference for multi-output supervised topic models with hierarchical prior. — stm_multi_hier_vi_parallel
text: model includes:   - LDA structure: theta_d ~ Dir(alpha), phi_k ~ Dir(beta)   - Gaussian response: y[d,j] ~ N(zbar_d^T eta_j, sigma_j^2)   - Hierarchical prior:       eta_j ~ N(mu, Lambda^-1)       Lambda ~ inverse-Wishart(upsilon, Omega)

title: Variational inference for multi-output supervised topic models with hierarchical prior. — stm_multi_hier_vi_parallel
code: stm_multi_hier_vi_parallel(   mod,   docs,   y,   ndsum,   NZ,   V,   K,   J,   alpha,   beta,   mu,   upsilon,   Omega,   update_sigma = TRUE,   tau = 20L,   exact_second_moment = FALSE,   show_progress = TRUE,   chunk = 5000L )

title: Variational inference for multi-output supervised topic models with hierarchical prior. — stm_multi_hier_vi_parallel
text: mod List model state: - nd  (D x K) document-topic counts - nw  (K x V) topic-word counts - eta (K x J) regression coefficients - sigma2 (J) noise variances docs IntegerMatrix (NZ x 3) (doc_id, word_id, count). y NumericMatrix (D x J) response matrix. ndsum IntegerVector (D) document token counts. NZ, V, K, J Model dimensions. alpha, beta Dirichlet hyperparameters. mu NumericVector (K) prior mean. upsilon Degrees freedom inverse-Wishart. Omega Scale matrix inverse-Wishart. update_sigma Logical; update sigma2 . tau Numeric cutoff stability. exact_second_moment Logical flag (currently used). show_progress Logical; print progress. chunk Integer; documents per parallel block.

title: Variational inference for multi-output supervised topic models with hierarchical prior. — stm_multi_hier_vi_parallel
text: list updated variational parameters diagnostics: nd D x K integer matrix document-topic counts. nw K x V integer matrix topic-word counts. eta K x J numeric matrix regression coefficients. sigma2 Length-J numeric vector noise variances. Lambda_E K x K numeric matrix, posterior mean precision matrix Lambda. IW_upsilon_hat Numeric scalar, posterior degrees freedom. IW_Omega_hat K x K numeric matrix, posterior scale matrix. elbo Numeric scalar, evidence lower bound. label_loglik Numeric scalar, supervised log-likelihood term.

what: Variational inference for supervised LDA (single continuous response). — stm_vi_parallel
title: Variational inference for supervised LDA (single continuous response). — stm_vi_parallel
text: model combines unsupervised topic modeling (LDA) Gaussian response document-level topic proportions.

title: Variational inference for supervised LDA (single continuous response). — stm_vi_parallel
code: stm_vi_parallel(   mod,   docs,   y,   ndsum,   NZ,   V,   K,   alpha,   beta,   update_sigma = TRUE,   tau = 20L,   show_progress = TRUE,   chunk = 5000L )

title: Variational inference for supervised LDA (single continuous response). — stm_vi_parallel
text: mod list containing current model state: nd D x K matrix document-topic counts. nw K x V matrix topic-word counts. eta Numeric vector length K; regression coefficients. sigma2 Scalar noise variance Gaussian response. docs IntegerMatrix size NZ x 3, row triple (d, v, c) 0-based indexing: document index d, word index v, count c = n_dv. Rows d outside [0, D-1] ignored. y NumericVector length D; response y_d document. ndsum IntegerVector length D; total token count per document (, ndsum[d] = sum_v n_dv). NZ Integer, number non-zero entries docs (rows docs). V Integer, vocabulary size. K Integer, number topics. alpha Scalar Dirichlet prior parameter document-topic distributions theta_d (symmetric prior parameter alpha). beta Scalar Dirichlet prior parameter topic-word distributions phi_k (symmetric prior parameter beta). update_sigma Logical; TRUE, update noise variance sigma2 residuals y_d - zbar_d^T eta, otherwise keep sigma2 fixed. tau Numeric, log-space cutoff used prune small topic responsibilities phi[d,,k] numerical stability efficiency. show_progress Logical; TRUE, print simple progress output E-step documents. chunk Integer, number documents process per parallel block E-step. Larger values reduce overhead may use memory.

title: Variational inference for supervised LDA (single continuous response). — stm_vi_parallel
text: list updated variational parameters diagnostics: nd Updated D x K document-topic counts. nw Updated K x V topic-word counts. eta Updated K-dimensional regression coefficient vector. sigma2 Updated scalar noise variance. elbo Scalar evidence lower bound (approximate). label_loglik Gaussian response log-likelihood component.

title: Variational inference for supervised LDA (single continuous response). — stm_vi_parallel
text: $$y_d \sim N(zbar_d^T eta, sigma^2).$$ function performs one variational inference sweep parallel document-level E-step simple updates regression parameters.
